question_text: What is the pKa of a sp hybridized carbon atom? A) 25 B) 35 C) 45 D) 50

A) Choose the letter of the molecule with the lowest barrier to rotation (or ring-flip). B) Select the letter of the molecule with the highest barrier to rotation (or ring-flip). A) ethane B) propane C) butane D) cyclohexane
Rank the following substituents in order of priority (1 = highest priority): -CH=CH2, -CN, -CH2NH2, -CH2Br.

If a chiral molecule has an absolute configuration of R, which direction does it rotate the plane of polarized light? A) clockwise (dextrorotary) B) counterclockwise (levarotatory) C) it doesn't rotate the plane of polarized light D) can't be determined from the information given

A) Enter the pKa value for each acid in the boxes below. B) Indicate whether the reactants or products will be favored at equilibrium by choosing the appropriate set of equilibrium arrows. (A longer arrow is drawn toward the species favored at equilibrium.) The first equilibrium involves phenol (C6H5OH) plus HO- ⇌ phenolate (C6H5O-) plus water (H2O) with boxes for pKa values under phenol and water. The second equilibrium shows acetate ion (CH3COO-) plus protonated methylamine (CH3NH3+) ⇌ acetic acid (CH3COOH) plus methylamine (CH3NH2) with boxes for pKa values under protonated methylamine and acetic acid.
canonical_answer: A) phenol pKa=8-11; water pKa=15.7; protonated methylamine pKa=10-11; acetic acid pKa=4-5; B) equilibrium arrows favor phenolate and water in first reaction (right side); equilibrium arrows favor acetic acid and methylamine in second reaction (right side)

error_comment: Handwritten structures and curved arrows in boxes for acid-base reactions are not fully clear for exact extraction.

question_text: Rank the following sets of molecules in order of acidity (1 = most acidic). The first set includes: protonated dimethylamine (H+N(CH3)2), protonated methyl thiol (H+S-CH3), protonated dimethylphosphine (H+P(CH3)2), protonated chloride (H+Cl), and protonated tert-butyl cation (H+C(CH3)3). The second set includes protonated thioacetic acid (CH3C(=O)SH with H+ on oxygen), protonated methanesulfenic acid (CH3C(=O)SCH3 with H+ on sulfur), protonated acetamide (CH3C(=O)NH2 with H+ on oxygen), protonated acetone (CH3C(=O)CH3 with H+ on oxygen), and protonated acetic acid (CH3C(=O)OH with H+ on oxygen). Boxes are placed below each molecule with numbers indicating rank: first set ranks 4, 2, 3, 1, 5; second set ranks 3, 1, 5, 2, 4.

Met-enkephalin, an endorphin, serves as a natural pain reliever that changes or removes the perception of nerve signals. Label all of the functional groups present in Met-enkephalin. The molecule shows: an amine group (H2N), phenol group (benzene ring with OH), amide groups (multiple occurrences of NH and C=O bonds), an arene (benzene ring), a carboxylic acid group (COOH), and a sulfide group (S bonded to CH3).
canonical_answer: amine; phenol; amide; arene; carboxylic acid; sulfide

question_text: Name the following molecules. 1) An alkane structure with groups labeled: 3-methyl, 4-isopropyl, 5-methyl, 6-methyl on an octane chain, combined into the name 4-isopropyl-3,5,6-trimethyloctane. 2) A substituted cycloheptane with a bromine (Br) substituent at position 1 and a tert-butyl group at position 4, named trans-1-bromo-4-tert-butylcycloheptane.

A) Draw the 4 major resonance contributors for the molecule shown in the first box. Partially completed structures are provided as a time-saver. Do not generate any additional charges. B) Draw in all lone pairs of electrons and use arrows to show the movement of electrons within the structure. C) Place a checkmark in the small boxes of the three structures that contribute the most to the resonance hybrid. D) Choose all the nucleophilic atoms in the molecule at the bottom of the page. The molecule at the bottom shows positions on a cyclopentyl ring attached to a carbonyl connected to a six-membered ring with an N-CH3 group, with circles around two oxygen atoms and the nitrogen atom.
canonical_answer: A) The four partially completed resonance contributor structures with curved arrows depicting electron movement as shown in the image, with no additional charges added; C) The three resonance structures with check marks in their indicated small boxes; D) The two oxygen atoms attached to the carbonyl and carboxyl groups, and the nitrogen atom attached to the CH3 group at the bottom molecule are nucleophilic atoms.

question_text: A) Choose the structures that represent a conformation of 2,2-dimethylbutane sighting along any C-C bond. Four Newman projections are shown with groups labeled as Me, tBu, Et, H in different orientations. B) Complete each of the Newman projections below to show the most stable and least stable conformations of 2,2-dimethylbutane, sighting along the C2-C3 bond. Two Newman projections with partial substituent labels H3C, CH3, and H are shown for completion. C) Use the above Newman projections to calculate the barrier to rotation of 2,2-dimethylbutane sighting along the C2-C3 bond.
canonical_answer: A) Two structures are chosen (the circled ones). B) Completed Newman projections with substituents arranged for most and least stable conformations along C2-C3 bond. C) Barrier to rotation = 3.6 kcal/mol

question_text: A) Draw in the substituents on the ring flipped conformers of each molecule (A and B) to predict which molecule is lower in energy. B) Show the Newman projection for each ring-flipped conformer, sighting along the C5-C6 and C3-C2 bonds. Molecule A and B are cyclohexane derivatives with substituents: methyl groups at carbons 1 and 2 and a chlorine at carbon 4, with specific stereochemistry shown on the chair conformations. C) Choose the chair conformation that is lower in energy for each molecule. D) Which structure is lower in energy (choose one)? A or B E) Briefly explain your choice.
canonical_answer: C) Chair conformation with equatorial methyl groups and axial Cl for each molecule as shown circled in the diagram; D) A; E) A has fewer 1,3-diaxial interactions in its lowest energy conformation

question_text: A) Label each molecule as chiral (C), achiral (A), or achiral/meso (M). Three molecules are shown: a bicyclic molecule with a CH3 substituent, a molecule with an NH2 group, Br, OH, and N (all attached to a tetrahedral carbon), and a cyclic molecule with two CH3 and two OH substituents arranged oppositely. B) Designate each chirality center as R or S. For the middle molecule, chirality centers near Br and OH are labeled with 'S' and 'R' corresponding to 'front' and 'back'.
canonical_answer: A) First molecule = A; Second molecule = C; Third molecule = M; B) Chirality centers: middle molecule - S (front), R (back)

question_text:
Indicate the relationships between the two molecules in each pair as enantiomers (E), diastereomers (D), or same molecule (S).
Pairs: 1) Two cyclic molecules each with OH groups attached on two adjacent carbons with opposite wedge/dash orientations. 2) Two cyclohexane chair-like conformations with Br, OH, CH3, and CH2OH groups arranged differently on carbons. 3) Two acyclic molecules with Br, CH3, OH, and ethyl substituents arranged differently with wedge/dash bonds, shown as stereoisomers.
canonical_answer: First pair: E; Second pair: E; Third pair: D

question_text: (4 pts) One of the steps in fat biosynthesis is the hydration of crotonate to yield 3-hydroxybutyrate. The reaction occurs by addition of -OH to the si face at C3, followed by protonation at C2, also from the si face. A) Label the top face of each alkene carbon atom as re or si. B) Show the stereochemistry of the product. C) Label C3 as R or S. The reaction scheme shows crotonate with carbons numbered 2 and 3; crotonate has notation "re" at carbon 3 and "si" at carbon 2 labeled. The product is 3-hydroxybutyrate with OH and H substituents at carbons 3 and 2 respectively. An additional hand-drawn structure is shown with OH and S labeling.
canonical_answer: A) Carbon 3 top face is re; Carbon 2 top face is si. B) The product stereochemistry is shown with OH and H groups added from the si face. C) C3 is labeled as S.
